mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate above 4.5% raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing below-average S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains at restrictive levels.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not statistically lower than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumption slowdowns, causing S&P 500 forward returns to be negative over the following quarter until the unemployment trend stabilizes.
failure_condition: Following triggered UNRATE increases, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect size is not distinguishable from baseline returns using a two-sample t-test (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST is declining while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, the joint tightening of credit and housing activity signals a broader demand contraction that compounds negatively on equity risk premia, producing weaker S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days than either signal alone would predict.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 20-day forward returns are not significantly more negative than returns conditioned on HOUST decline alone (difference-in-means test p > 0.05), or interaction term loses explanatory power relative to additive single-factor model in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion regime, continued acceleration in INDPRO signals capacity utilization pressure and margin compression risk for producers, leading to muted or negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter even though industrial output growth appears strong.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth > 2% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: In late-cycle periods with INDPRO growth above threshold, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive and statistically indistinguishable from early-cycle returns (p > 0.05), invalidating the late-cycle margin-compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week whenever retail sales momentum is positive.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive RSAFS momentum are not significantly higher than returns following negative momentum (t-test p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in PAYEMS exceeding 100,000 jobs signals an abrupt labor market inflection that triggers risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days following the release.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns after PAYEMS declines exceeding threshold show no significant negative skew relative to baseline distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated (year-over-year > 3%) while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously high, the combination signals a sustained restrictive monetary stance that the market has not fully priced, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter until either inflation or rates decline meaningfully.
failure_condition: Joint high-inflation/high-rate regime shows 60-day forward returns statistically equal to or greater than unconditional average returns (p > 0.05), or the interaction adds no incremental predictive power beyond either series individually in walk-forward variance decomposition.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, sequential quarterly increases in GDPC1 signal reacceleration of aggregate output that supports forward earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as growth data confirms recovery durability.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: During periods meeting the GDPC1 growth trigger within a recovery regime, 60-day forward SPX returns fail to exceed the unconditional historical mean return by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05), or GDPC1 data revisions materially alter the sign of the growth trigger in more than 20% of observed instances.
